segments.text: Job continued his parable, and said:
Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days when God preserved me;
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: continued: Heb. added to take up

segments.text: Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days when God preserved me;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God was upon my tabernacle;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were about me;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I went out to the gate through the city, when I prepared my seat in the street!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the aged arose, and stood up.
The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.
The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.

footnotes: The nobles…: Heb. The voice of the nobles was hid

segments.text: When the ear heard me, then it blessed me; and when the eye saw me, it gave witness to me:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me: my judgment was as a robe and a diadem.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I was a father to the poor: and the cause which I knew not I searched out.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: And I brake the jaws of the wicked, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: the jaws: Heb. the jawteeth, or, the grinders

segments.text: Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My root was spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night upon my branch.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My glory was fresh in me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I laughed on them, they believed it not; and the light of my countenance they cast not down.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I chose out their way, and sat chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth the mourners.
segments.speaker: Job
